title: MD
biography: Gao Yaojie was a Chinese gynecologist who became one of China's most prominent HIV/AIDS activists. In the 1990s she exposed the blood-selling scandal in Henan province that infected hundreds of thousands of rural Chinese with HIV. Despite government persecution, she continued to advocate for victims and educate the public about AIDS prevention.
aiSummary: Gao Yaojie was a gynecology specialist and HIV/AIDS whistleblower who exposed the Henan blood-selling scandal that infected hundreds of thousands. Based in Henan, China.

timeline.title: Discovered HIV epidemic from blood selling
timeline.description: Identified widespread HIV infections in Henan province caused by unsanitary commercial blood collection

timeline.title: Jonathan Mann Award
timeline.description: Recognized for courage in exposing the HIV crisis despite government pressure

timeline.title: Ramon Magsaysay Award
timeline.description: Received Asia's Nobel Prize equivalent for public health advocacy

timeline.title: Fled to the United States
timeline.description: Left China after years of house arrest and government persecution for her activism
